reasoning: Query is about cherry blossom spots in Tohoku (not Tokyo). Tool fetches Akita spots and the data does contain Kakunodate (sakura_meisho_100, famous for weeping cherry) at position 4 with explicit blossom description. However the response leads with unrelated spots (Lake Tazawa, Mount Chokai, fireworks competition) before the cherry-relevant entry. The data is present but the synthesis fails to prioritize sakura-tagged items. Also, Tohoku spans 6 prefectures — only Akita was fetched.
missing_data: Other Tohoku prefectures (Aomori Hirosaki, Iwate Kitakami, Miyagi Funaoka) are famous cherry spots not retrieved. No seasonal timing info. Sakura-specific ranking absent.
improvement_hint: Filter or rank by sakura-related kind tags (sakura_meisho_100) before returning results. For multi-prefecture region queries, fetch all relevant prefectures or use search_area with sakura keyword across Tohoku region codes.

reasoning: Query asks for major sake breweries in Niigata. get_local_specialty returns 24 items including the three great Niigata sake brands (越乃寒梅, 久保田, 八海山) plus the collective 越の三梅. Descriptions are well-translated with provenance. Minor issue: non-sake items also appear (くろさき茶豆 edamame GI is visible in truncated response) which dilutes focus, but the primary request is well-served. Brewery visit logistics are absent but that is outside MCP scope.
missing_data: Physical brewery locations/addresses, visiting hours, or tour availability. Daiginjo/junmai category breakdowns absent but beyond MCP scope.
improvement_hint: Add a sake-specific q-filter or kind_tag filter so get_local_specialty with a sake query surfaces only sake brands/breweries without edamame GI items dominating. Consider exposing filter by kind_tag.

reasoning: Query explicitly asks for ski resorts with onsen in Hakuba. The response correctly returns Hakuba Happo-One ski resort (with olympics context) as first result but then pivots to a UN Tourism award page, an alpine lake in a different municipality, an art museum, and a botanical garden. No onsen spot appears in the truncated response — the constraint 'with onsen' is not addressed. The tool lacks onsen-ski co-occurrence filtering.
missing_data: Onsen facilities in Hakuba (Mimizuku no Yu, Happo no Yu, etc.) are absent. No thermal spring kind tag items surfaced.
improvement_hint: Add onsen kind tags to the spots index for Hakuba area. Allow compound kind filtering (ski_resort AND onsen or nearby_onsen flag) in get_spots so dual-constraint queries can be answered.

reasoning: Query asks specifically for autumn foliage spots in Kansai. Kyoto is the right prefecture but none of the returned spots have koyo/autumn-foliage kind tags — Nijo Castle (castle), Toji Temple (temple), Nagaoka-kyo (archaeological), Sanzen-in (temple) appear in order. While Kyoto is the world's premier autumn foliage destination and these spots do have autumn color, the response is unfiltered by season/purpose. Kansai also includes Nara, Osaka, Hyogo — not fetched.
missing_data: Koyo-tagged spots (Eikan-do, Tofuku-ji, Arashiyama) not surfaced. Nara, Osaka, Hyogo autumn spots absent. Peak timing information missing.
improvement_hint: Index koyo/autumn_foliage as kind tags from DMO content. For region-level queries (Kansai = multiple prefectures), fetch all relevant prefectures and rank by seasonal relevance tags.

reasoning: Arabic query about famous cherry blossom places in Kansai. Tool fetches Nara which is a core Kansai prefecture with an excellent cherry spot: Mount Yoshino appears first with sakura_meisho_100 tag and 'Historic Site / Natural Monument' designations — this is Japan's most famous cherry mountain and directly answers the intent. Additional Nara spots (temples, tombs) are less sakura-focused but are real heritage sites. Coverage limited to one Kansai prefecture.
missing_data: Osaka (Kema Sakuranomiya), Kyoto (Maruyama, Philosopher's Path), Hyogo sakura spots not fetched. Multi-prefecture Kansai coverage absent.
improvement_hint: For Kansai region queries, batch-fetch Kyoto, Osaka, Nara, Hyogo and filter/rank by sakura_meisho_100 kind tag to surface the best cherry spots across the full region.

reasoning: Query asks specifically for remote Okinawan islands (離島) with beautiful starry skies. The response leads with Nakagusuku Castle (Okinawa main island) and Tamaudun/Shikina-en in Naha — none of these are remote islands or stargazing spots. Taketomi Island does appear (island kind tag) but no stargazing/dark-sky kind tag exists. The constraint 'remote island + stargazing' is almost entirely ignored.
missing_data: Iriomote, Yonaguni, Miyako, Kume Island stargazing spots absent. No dark-sky or astronomy kind tags. Ishigaki Island (certified stargazing destination) not surfaced.
improvement_hint: Add stargazing/dark_sky kind tags to relevant Yaeyama islands. For island-specific queries, filter by island kind tag and municipality within Okinawa. Consider a seasonal/night-activity dimension in the data model.

reasoning: Thai query about rural Hokkaido onsen accommodations. get_hotels returns confirmed hito_yu_kai (Nihon Hito-yu wo Mamoru Kai) onsen ryokan which are exactly 'rural onsen' properties — 知内温泉旅館, 銀婚湯, 湯元凌雲閣, 湯元湧駒荘 are all remote rural onsen. The constraint is well-matched. Downside: no coordinates for hito_yu_kai entries, no English names, and city hotels (ANA Holiday Inn Sapporo) appear mixed in.
missing_data: Coordinates and English names for hito_yu_kai properties absent. No price range for ryokan. Rural vs urban distinction not filterable.
improvement_hint: Geocode and add English names to hito_yu_kai properties. Add an is_rural flag or distance-from-urban-center score so rural-specific queries can filter out city hotels like ANA Holiday Inn.

reasoning: Vietnamese query about islands in the Seto Inland Sea worth visiting. get_spots for Kagawa returns Ritsurin Garden, Mannō Lake, Marugame Castle, Takamatsu Castle — all mainland Kagawa attractions. The Seto Inland Sea islands (Naoshima, Shodoshima, Teshima, Ogijima) are the specific intent. No island kind tag items appear in the truncated response for Kagawa, and Hiroshima/Okayama prefecture islands are also not covered.
missing_data: Naoshima (art island), Shodoshima, Teshima, Ogijima, Inujima not surfaced. Hiroshima's Itsukushima (Miyajima) and smaller Setouchi islands absent.
improvement_hint: For island queries in Kagawa, prioritize island kind-tagged results. Expand to multi-prefecture for Setouchi region (Hiroshima, Okayama, Ehime). A dedicated Setouchi island cluster in the index would improve recall significantly.

reasoning: Query asks for indigo dyeing workshops in Tokushima. The first result (阿波正藍しじら織) is directly on-topic as Awa Hon-ai Shijira Weave (authentic Tokushima indigo weaving), but description_ja/description are null — no workshop info. The third item (阿波和紙) mentions indigo-dyed washi but is paper craft, not workshops. The second item (大谷焼 ceramics) is off-topic. No workshop location info exists. The response provides traditional craft designations but not workshop access.
missing_data: Workshop venues, addresses, booking info. Awa Ai (indigo) experience centers in Tokushima city. The flagship 阿波藍 craft should have a description.
improvement_hint: Fill missing description_ja/description for meti_densan:0122 (阿波正藍しじら織). Add workshop venue links from official craft association sites. Consider a workshop_available boolean field for craft records.

reasoning: German query about Buddhist temples with lodging (Shukubo) on Mount Koya. The response returns confirmed Koyasan Shukubo Association members with phone numbers and rich Japanese descriptions of each temple. This is an exact match — 安養院, 一乗院, 惠光院, 北室院, 熊谷寺 are all legitimate Koyasan shukubo. The data is authoritative (source: koyasan_shukubo association). Minor gap: no English names/descriptions and no coordinates.
missing_data: English descriptions and names for shukubo entries. Coordinates absent for navigation. Booking URL per temple.
improvement_hint: Translate shukubo description_ja to English using the batch translation pipeline. Add coordinates from the Koyasan Shukubo Association pages. Add booking_url field from shukubo.net individual pages.

reasoning: Query asks for samurai districts outside Kyoto. search_area returns 146 matches. Visible results include: Shimane (Matsue buke yashiki), Fukui (Uchiyama family residence), Akita (Kakunodate — excellent choice, famous but not Kyoto), Kagoshima (Chiran samurai residences), and Aizu (Fukushima). These are well-distributed non-Kyoto examples from different regions. Kakunodate and Chiran are notable, genuinely off-Kyoto samurai districts. Good geographic spread.
missing_data: Kanazawa Nagamachi samurai district (Ishikawa) not visible in truncated results. No visit duration or seasonal info integrated.
improvement_hint: Ensure Kanazawa Nagamachi (石川県武家屋敷) is indexed with buke_yashiki kind tag. Add a 'not_in' prefecture filter capability so agents can explicitly exclude Kyoto from results.

reasoning: Indonesian query about summer festivals in Akita. get_festivals returns 3 items: Dainichido Bugaku (ritual dances — not a summer matsuri), Kakunodate Festival (autumn matsuri, usually September), Tsuchizaki Shinmei Shrine Festival (July — this one is summer-appropriate). The national_heritage section includes items from Iwate (Hayachine Kagura) which is not Akita. The count is only 3 and none have confirmed summer dates. Most critically, Akita's famous Kanto Festival (August) and Nebuta-like festivals are absent.
missing_data: Akita Kanto Festival (August), Omagari Fireworks (August), Noshiro Festival. Summer (July-August) date filter not applied.
wrong_data: Hayachine Kagura in national_heritage section is from Iwate (岩手県), not Akita — scope error.
improvement_hint: Add a season/month filter to get_festivals so 'summer' queries surface July-August dated events. Fix national_heritage scope so it doesn't include neighboring-prefecture ICH items. Expand festival coverage with DMO-scraped summer event data for Akita.

reasoning: Korean query about small fishing port towns in the San'in region. get_spots for Shimane returns Lake Shinji, Matsue Castle, Iwami Ginzan, Mount Sanbe — none are fishing port towns. The San'in region spans Shimane and Tottori, and fishing village spots like Omori, Yunotsu, Kotoura, Oki Islands are absent from the visible results. The constraint 'fishing port / harbor town' is entirely unaddressed.
missing_data: Yunotsu (historic port town near Iwami Ginzan), Oki Islands fishing villages, Kotoura/Sakaiminato port towns, Tottori's fishing harbors entirely absent.
improvement_hint: Add fishing_port / harbor_town kind tags to relevant Shimane/Tottori coastal municipalities. Expand tool scope for San'in region to include both prefectures. Index Oki Islands as island+fishing destination.

reasoning: Query asks about washi paper making experiences in Gifu. The response lists 8 craft items for Gifu but shows ceramics (Mino Ware), lacquerware (Hida Shunkei), and wood lathe work before any washi item. While 本美濃紙 (Hon Mino Paper — UNESCO ICH) is likely in the full result, it isn't visible in the truncated response. The tool returns all crafts without prioritizing washi, so the intent is addressed only partially.
missing_data: 本美濃紙 (Hon Mino Paper, UNESCO ICH 2014) not visible. Workshop/experience venue addresses for washi making absent. Mino Washi Museum not linked.
improvement_hint: For washi-specific queries, rank items with washi industry_category first. Add experience venue cross-reference (museum/workshop) to washi craft records. Ensure 本美濃紙 appears early in results when washi is the query topic.

reasoning: Spanish query about fishing villages on the Noto Peninsula. get_spots for Ishikawa returns Kanazawa Castle, Kenroku-en, and Bed and Craft hotel — all Kanazawa city attractions. Noto Peninsula fishing villages (Sosogi, Wajima, Suzu, Nozomi) are the specific intent. Nanao Castle does appear (Noto-related) but no fishing village kind tags are visible. The query constraint 'Noto Peninsula fishing villages' is almost completely missed.
missing_data: Wajima morning market, Sosogi coast, Noto satoyama satoumi (UNESCO GIAHS), Kiriko festival villages, Suzu coastline fishing hamlets entirely absent.
improvement_hint: Add Noto-specific municipality filter support and fishing_village/harbor_town kind tags. Wajima, Suzu, Noto Town should be indexed as Noto Peninsula municipalities with appropriate kind tags to surface on peninsula-specific queries.

reasoning: Query asks for lesser-known sand dunes beyond Tottori. search_area returns 90 results including Tottori (as expected) plus Onjuku Beach (Chiba), Ishikari sand dune museum (Hokkaido), Fukiagehama (Kagoshima), Tsujido Beach (Kanagawa), and a Kyoto mountain. Geographic diversity is good. However some entries are beaches with sand dune tags rather than actual dunes (Tsujido Beach), and the Ishikari entry is a museum rather than a dune itself. Still, the response surfaces genuinely diverse non-Tottori options.
missing_data: Nakatajima Sand Dunes (Hamamatsu, Shizuoka) and Sarobetsu sand dunes (Hokkaido) absent. Actual dune size/prominence data would help ranking.
wrong_data: Ishikari entry is a museum (いしかり砂丘の風資料館) not the dunes themselves — the dune tag is present but the entity is a museum.
improvement_hint: Distinguish actual sand dune landforms from museums/facilities tagged with sand_dune kind. Add prominence or area size to dune records. Ensure Nakatajima Sand Dunes (天竜浜名湖) are indexed.

reasoning: Chinese query about traditional crafts of the San'in region. get_local_specialty for Shimane returns 4 METI-designated crafts in well-translated Chinese: 石見焼 (Iwami Ware ceramics), 石州和紙 (Sekishu Washi), 雲州そろばん (Unshū abacus), 出雲石燈ろう (Izumo stone lanterns). All four are authentic Shimane crafts with high-quality Chinese descriptions. The see_also section includes Iwami Ginzan. Excellent coverage for the query. Only Tottori (the other San'in prefecture) is missing.
missing_data: Tottori crafts (因州和紙, 弓浜絣) not included since only Shimane was queried. San'in as a two-prefecture region not fully covered.
improvement_hint: For San'in region queries, automatically include both Shimane and Tottori prefecture results. Add a region parameter to get_local_specialty to handle multi-prefecture regional identities.

reasoning: Query asks specifically for lavender fields in Hokkaido beyond Furano. get_spots for Hokkaido (limit 50) returns Showa-Shinzan volcano, Sapporo Clock Tower, Matsumae Castle, Daisetsuzan — none are lavender-related. Furano itself (the famous lavender area) isn't shown either. No lavender/flower-field kind tags exist in the data. The specific floral tourism constraint is completely missed.
missing_data: Furano Flower Fields, Tomita Farm, Nakafurano lavender, Biei flower fields, Kamifurano. No lavender or flower_field kind tags in corpus.
improvement_hint: Add lavender/flower_field kind tags to Furano, Biei, and Kamifurano entries. Index Tomita Farm and other notable flower farms. Consider a seasonal flower tourism dimension in kind tags to surface seasonal attraction queries.

reasoning: Filipino query about traditional old towns (古い町並み) in Kyushu. get_spots for Oita returns Usuki Castle, Usa Jingū, Oka Castle, and a Kokonoe tourism association page — none are traditional townscape preservation districts. Oita does have Usuki traditional townscape and Hita Mameda-machi merchant town, but these aren't surfaced. The query intent 'traditional town streetscapes' is not addressed.
missing_data: Hita Mameda-machi (商家町), Usuki historical townscape, Oita's preservation districts. Kyushu old towns in Nagasaki (Dejima area), Kagoshima (Chiran), Fukuoka (Yanagawa) also absent.
improvement_hint: Add preservation_district and jokamachi kind tags more comprehensively for Oita. For Kyushu old town queries, a multi-prefecture approach covering Nagasaki, Kumamoto, Kagoshima would improve recall.

reasoning: Query asks about kominka (traditional farmhouse) stays in the Hokuriku region. get_hotels for Ishikawa returns hito_yu_kai onsen ryokan (not kominka), and a 'Student housing 1' OSM entry — which is clearly not a travel accommodation. Bed and Craft kominka hotel (shown in get_spots for Ishikawa in L2-20) is not appearing here. The query constraint 'kominka stay' is partially addressed by onsen ryokan as a category but actual kominka hotels are missing.
missing_data: Bed and Craft (kominka hotel network in Toyama/Ishikawa) not in hotel results. Noto Peninsula kominka renovations absent. Fukui/Toyama kominka lodging not fetched.
wrong_data: Student housing 1 (OSM node cluster) is a data quality issue — not a traveler accommodation.
improvement_hint: Filter OSM entries with student_housing or non-tourism use types from hotel results. Add kominka_stay as a lodging_type. Cross-link get_spots kominka-tagged entries to get_hotels results. Query should span Ishikawa, Toyama, Fukui for full Hokuriku coverage.

reasoning: Query asks about mountain shrines on the Kii Peninsula. get_japan_heritage for Wakayama returns a well-structured response with Kongōbu-ji Temple (Mt. Koya), Kumano Kodō pilgrimage routes, and Kumano Sanzan shrines — all directly relevant mountain-worship (山岳信仰) heritage. The tool_scope_note correctly guides the agent to also check get_traditional_arts for UNESCO WHS context. Excellent source authority (National Treasure + UNESCO WHS designations). Negoro-ji also surfaces. Strong response.
missing_data: Yoshino-Kumano National Park mountain shrine details, specific Hongu/Nachi/Hayatama Taisha individual entries could be more granular.
improvement_hint: Add individual Kumano Sanzan shrine entries (Hongu Taisha, Nachi Taisha, Hayatama Taisha) as separate wikidata_heritage records for more granular mountain shrine responses.

reasoning: Query asks about summer fireworks festivals along the Sea of Japan coast. get_festivals for Niigata returns 129 items but the visible truncated results show: Uonuma moss phlox festival (May), Himesakuri mountain hiking festival (May), Nagaoka Grand Fireworks (August — this is relevant!), Niigata Canal Sai event. The Nagaoka fireworks is directly on topic. However, the Sea of Japan coast constraint goes beyond Niigata — Akita, Yamagata, Ishikawa also have coastal fireworks. The top items are seasonal flower events, not fireworks.
missing_data: Noshiro Port Fireworks (Akita), Tsuruoka Fireworks, Kanazawa Port fireworks, coastal Sea of Japan fireworks beyond Niigata. Omagari (Akita) was in Case L2-06 context.
improvement_hint: Add a hanabi/fireworks filter to get_festivals so fireworks-specific queries can surface only relevant events. For Sea of Japan coast queries, batch-fetch Niigata, Akita, Yamagata, Ishikawa prefectures.

reasoning: Query asks about traditional bamboo crafts in Oita. get_local_specialty returns 2 items: Hakata Ori weaving (multi-prefecture including Oita — not bamboo) and Beppu Bamboo Crafts (別府竹細工 — directly on target, with excellent description of Madake bamboo and eight weaving techniques). The bamboo craft item has high-quality provenance and production area details. Hakata Ori is tangential but technically Oita-designated. See_also venues include relevant Oita museums.
missing_data: Workshop/experience venues for Beppu bamboo craft not linked. Beppu Takekougeikan (Bamboo Craft Museum) address absent.
improvement_hint: Cross-link Beppu Bamboo Craft Museum (大分県立竹工芸訓練センター) as a see_also venue. Add experience_available flag to craft records where hands-on workshops exist at designated production areas.

reasoning: Query asks about cycling routes through rural Shikoku. get_spots for Ehime returns Ozu Castle, Yuzuki Castle, Omishima Island (cycling_route kind tag!), Uwajima Castle. Omishima is part of the Shimanami Kaidō cycling route — highly relevant. However cycling routes require Hiroshima and other Shikoku prefectures too, and the response buries the cycling-relevant spot at position 3 behind two castles. The Shimanami Kaidō as a whole is not surfaced as a route-level entity.
missing_data: Shimanami Kaidō route entity, Imabari cycling infrastructure, Kochi and Tokushima rural cycling spots. Cycling-specific distance/elevation data absent.
improvement_hint: Create a route-level entity for Shimanami Kaidō with cycling_route kind tag. Prioritize cycling_route kind-tagged spots in ranked results when cycling is the primary query intent. Expand to all four Shikoku prefectures for rural cycling queries.

reasoning: Query asks where to see kabuki outside Tokyo and Kyoto. get_traditional_arts returns 183 items but the visible results are: Bunraku (puppet theatre — not kabuki), Kabuki (generic UNESCO-level entry with no venue info), Chakkirako (Kanagawa dance), Dainichido Bugaku (Akita). The Kabuki entry is present but has no location or venue data. Regional kabuki venues (Iya Kabuki in Tokushima, Chichibu Yomatsuri, Kōchi Sakura-kai) are not surfaced. The constraint 'outside Tokyo/Kyoto + specific venue' is unresolved.
missing_data: Regional kabuki venues: Iya no Kakashi kabuki (Tokushima), Nagahama Kabuki (Shiga), Chichibu Yomatsuri kabuki (Saitama), venue addresses and performance schedules.
improvement_hint: Add prefecture-level venue tags to kabuki entries in the traditional arts index. Include regional folk kabuki (地芝居) as distinct entries from grand kabuki. A venue-type filter would help distinguish performance art forms from location-based queries.
